goal_scope: Add a higher-functionality browser-local Midday Rescue workflow to the public-safe LifeMax app and deploy it to Vercel.
truth_boundary: This proof covers browser-local demo behavior only.
WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, backend writes, shared-context daily usability, and medical/wellness-intensity automation are still not connected or claimed.

Production deployment was approved earlier in this goal with PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote; no credential, auth setting, DNS/custom-domain, backend, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, or WHOOP mutation was performed.
The latest LifeMax lab preflight and boot packet remain red, so the broader LifeMax operating loop is not complete.
product_gap_evidence: docs/LIFEMAX_LAUNCH_APP_MASTER_PLAN.md describes Midday Rescue as a core MVP loop: user reports drift and gets one reset plus a lower-intensity next move.
docs/LIFEMAX_PRODUCT_PIVOT_STRATEGY.md says drift should produce one reset rather than a lecture.
Live /today before this repair did not expose a Midday Rescue workflow; a Playwright text probe returned hasMidday:false.

assumptions_and_risk_repairs.assumption: Adding more UI without a real state-changing flow would produce fake confidence.
assumptions_and_risk_repairs.repair: Implemented persisted LocalDemoState.midday_rescue, saved rescue evidence, command-center status, Profile/export summary, Privacy copy, and Playwright assertions that the form values persist after refresh.

assumptions_and_risk_repairs.assumption: A rescue flow could imply backend reminders or clinical advice.
assumptions_and_risk_repairs.repair: Copy states this is browser-local only, no reminder/backend write is sent, and the action is a lower-intensity planning aid rather than a medical claim.

assumptions_and_risk_repairs.assumption: The form could look saved while draft fields hydrate incorrectly after refresh.
assumptions_and_risk_repairs.repair: Visual inspection found stale rescue/evening-close drafts after refresh; keyed panels to persisted state and added Playwright value assertions.

defects_found: No visible Midday Rescue surface existed on /today despite product docs treating it as a core daily loop.
Initial local visual rerun after adding the flow found saved status with stale/blank rescue and evening-close draft fields after refresh.
A first production smoke assertion was too brittle because privacy copy contained the phrase midday rescue in two places; the scoped assertion was repaired and rerun.

repairs_made.summary: Added MiddayRescue types, normalization, derived rescue_summary, export summary rescue status, local memory source, day-review evidence contribution, and risk flag integration.

Added the Today Midday Rescue panel with trigger/reset controls, lower-intensity move, defer-until, note, save/update action, command-center and workflow-rail rescue status, Profile summary, Privacy copy, and draft hydration keys.
Expanded the workflow rail to five steps and added restrained rescue-panel styling that fits the existing calm app-shell design.
Added unit and Playwright coverage for open/saved rescue derivation, save flow, refresh persistence, Profile/export visibility, Privacy copy, and the stale-draft regression.

local_checks.summary: tsc --noEmit passed after Midday Rescue model/UI updates.
eslint passed after Midday Rescue model/UI updates.
Rerun exposed stale rescued/close draft hydration after refresh; after keyed-panel repair, Next build plus Playwright visual smoke passed: 2 tests.
No whitespace errors in the final code diff.
No secret-shaped values found; matches were only public truth-boundary words.

browser_flows_proven: Today shows Rescue in status cards, command center, and the five-step workflow rail.
Before check-in, Rescue is honestly locked with a disabled save path and local-only copy.
After manual check-in, user can save a Midday Rescue with trigger, one reset, lower-intensity next move, defer-until, and note.
Saving rescue lowers today intensity locally, writes a local memory candidate, updates Today status, and keeps buttons functional rather than decorative.
Rescue state and input values persist after refresh through localStorage.
Profile shows Rescue: saved and the export preview includes midday_rescue plus the rescue note.
Privacy is reachable and now names daily plan, midday rescue, evening close, review notes, and experiment observations as browser-local data.
Production mobile smoke on both aliases measured scrollWidth 390 and clientWidth 390, with no console/page/HTTP 500 errors captured.

git.note: This proof packet is written after the deployed code commit; the final proof-only commit hash is recorded in the Codex final response to avoid self-referential packet churn.

production_smoke.script_summary: Playwright shell smoke drove Today check-in, plan save, Midday Rescue save, refresh persistence, Profile/export preview, and Privacy on both production aliases; no serious browser errors were captured.

independent_verification.note: quality_verifier inspected the Midday Rescue proof, repo source/tests, lab boot truth boundary, Vercel aliases, live behavior, and screenshots. It passed this as a public browser-local demo checkpoint and explicitly rejected broader COMPLETE_OK because shared-context/backend/WHOOP/Telegram/n8n/public MCP/Grok/Postgres remain red or unproved.
commands_rerun_by_verifier: fresh Playwright live smoke on life-max-omega and lifemax-web
vercel inspect for both production aliases
disqualifying_gaps_for_complete: Lab-side LifeMax local agent context preflight and boot packet remain red.
get_life_safety_state and get_grok_context_brief are not loaded green.
No proof exists for WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, backend writes, or shared-context daily usability.
Vercel metadata was not sufficient to tie aliases directly to the git hash; live behavior, pushed origin/main, deployment readiness, and smoke results are the practical proof.

stop_continue_decision: checkpoint, not complete: this continuation raises public browser-local Today functionality and deploys it, but full LifeMax shared-context/backend/daily-use readiness remains red and unproved.
next_action: Commit and push this proof checkpoint; if continuing the broader goal, choose either another visible local product workflow repair or a separate backend/shared-context Wave 1 repair after local MCP/shared-context reads are green.
